title: fam-max-loop — the max-reduction max_j x[j] over a buffer emits four-way as Form->asm bytes AND runs bit-exact on the M4; the HEADLINE consumer of fa-fmax (stable-softmax's max-subtract), a pure composition brick (no new encoder)
author: Sema (Opus 4.8), autonomous native-ML walk
stone: form-native-models.form 'GAPS — LLM INFERENCE': the nonlinear/reduction ops as form-asm bytes. fam-relu (#3937) proved fa-fmax as a SCALAR activation and named this exact brick in its receipt (next_stones[2]: 'a fam-softmax-max reduction: fold fa-fmax over an ll-buffer of logits (max_j) ... fa-fmax's headline consumer'). This brick folds fa-fmax across a whole buffer: max_j x[j], which is exactly stable softmax's max-subtract — softmax(x)_i = exp(x_i - m)/sum_j exp(x_j - m) with m = max_j x[j], so the largest exponent is 0 and exp never overflows. Every attention block runs this reduction before its softmax; it is the numerically-critical half of the softmax the native asm lane still lacked. The matvec lane is on bytes (form-asm-matvec-2d 127), the scalar activation is on bytes (form-asm-relu 15), and now the max-reduction the softmax needs is on bytes too.
new_encoder: NONE — this is a PURE COMPOSITION brick. fam-max-loop reuses only encoders proven in prior bands: fa-ldr-d (form-asm-float 2047), fa-movz-x / fa-cmp-r / fa-bcond / fa-add-x-imm / fa-b-off (form-asm-matvec-loop 127), fa-fmax (form-asm-relu 15), fa-ret. The reusable win is structural: the counted accumulate-loop discipline (init acc, cmp/b.ge exit, fold body, pointer-increment, b back-edge) is now shown to carry a fmax fold as cleanly as it carried the dot (fam-dot-loop) and the sum-of-squares (fam-ss-sqrt) — one loop skeleton, the fold op swaps.
recipe: form/form-stdlib/form-asm-matvec.fk :: (defn fam-max-loop () (append (fa-ldr-d 0 0 0) (append (fa-movz-x 3 0) (append (fa-cmp-r 3 1) (append (fa-bcond 10 6) (append (fa-ldr-d 1 0 0) (append (fa-fmax 0 0 1) (append (fa-add-x-imm 0 0 8) (append (fa-add-x-imm 3 3 1) (append (fa-b-off -6) (fa-ret))))))))))) — the ONE structural difference from fam-ss-sqrt: the accumulator inits to x[0] via `ldr d0,[x0]` (the max identity for n>=1) rather than 0.0 (movz+scvtf), because 0.0 would be WRONG for all-negative logits (a real softmax input after a bias/mask). No -inf constant to materialize (toolchain-pure); the loop folds fmax over all n including x[0] again (fmax(x0,x0)=x0, idempotent, no n=0 special case). ABI: x0 = x base, w1 = n (n>=1), returns d0 = max_j x[j]. 10 arm64 instructions = 40 bytes.

proof_four_way.oracle: M4-assembled words (otool -t of the exact 10-instruction sequence): fd400000 (ldr d0,[x0]) d2800003 (mov x3,#0) 6b01007f (cmp w3,w1) 540000ca (b.ge +6) fd400001 (ldr d1,[x0]) 1e614800 (fmax d0,d0,d1) 91002000 (add x0,#8) 91000463 (add x3,#1) 17fffffa (b -6) d65f03c0 (ret) = 40 bytes.
claims: 2 — the FULL 40-byte program byte-equals the M4-assembled oracle (fa-conviction == 1)
4 — fmax d0,d0,d1 (1e614800) — the max-fold, the headline fa-fmax consumer, byte-exact (fa-conviction == 1)
8 — ldr d0,[x0] (fd400000) — accumulator INIT to x[0] (max identity, not 0.0), byte-exact (fa-conviction == 1)
16 — b.ge +6 exit branch (scaled offset to the ret), byte-exact (fa-conviction == 1)
32 — b -6 back-edge (26-bit signed offset to the compare), byte-exact (fa-conviction == 1)
proof_four_way.result: -> 63 ; fourth arm four-way (fkwu, bootstrap binary darwin-arm64, no clang) ; 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go=Rust=TS=fkwu agree on the 40-byte image. Crossed FIRST try (the composed encoders were all pre-proven; the only new surface is the branch offsets ±6 and the x[0] init, both pinned against the M4 oracle before authoring).

proof_on_hardware.path: Form kernel emits fam-max-loop's 40-byte program -> form-macho mo-object-sym -> Mach-O .o (273 bytes) exporting _recipe -> ld -dylib + ad-hoc sign -> dlopen+call. No rustc/clang in the COMPUTE (clang only builds the dumb dlopen harness; the reduction is the Form-emitted loop). otool of the .o confirms the words disassemble to the exact ldr/mov/cmp/b.ge/ldr/fmax/add/add/b/ret loop (the assembler is the byte oracle).
proof_on_hardware.result: 6/6 MATCH on M4 (arm64 macOS): recipe(x, n) == max_j x[j] bit-for-bit. Cases: {3,1,4,1,5,9,2,6}->9 (peak mid-buffer); {-2,-7,-3,-0.5}->-0.5 (ALL negative — the decisive case: a 0.0 init would wrongly return 0, this proves the x[0] init); {8,2,5,1}->8 (peak is the first element/init); {-1,2,-3,4,-5}->4 (mixed signs); {42.5}->42.5 (n=1, the loop degenerates correctly); a 1000-element buffer with peak 314.159 at index 737 -> 314.159 (the counted loop scales).
proof_on_hardware.note: The headline fa-fmax consumer is now sovereign native bytes. This is stable-softmax's max-subtract — the numerically-critical reduction every attention block runs before its softmax — running bit-exact on the M4, no rustc/clang in the compute.

no_regression: fam-max-loop is purely additive to form-asm-matvec.fk (a new recipe reusing existing encoders); the band and manifest row are additive; no encoder changed. The scoped four-way validate loads form-asm.fk + form-asm-matvec.fk and crosses -> 63 with 0 divergent, which re-exercises the sibling recipes' shared prelude. No byte in any existing recipe moved.
design_note: Design-with-reference held: the exact 10-instruction sequence was assembled with clang -c on THIS M4 and read back with otool BEFORE authoring, pinning every word (including the ±6 branch offsets and the fd400000 x[0]-init load) against the ground-truth bytes; the emitter then composes pre-proven encoders to reproduce them, and the band proves byte-identity four-way. READ-THE-BODY held (the 30-somethingth time): this brick was the LITERAL next_stones[2] of the just-merged fam-relu receipt, and its loop twin fam-ss-sqrt supplied the entire skeleton — the only genuinely new decision was the accumulator init (x[0], not 0.0, for the max identity). Uncontested-stone discipline (lane-5): the exp-lane transcendental frontier (sibling claude/form-asm-exp-reduce, merged) and full-llama-generation (codex/full-real-llama-generation-d617) and Metal GPU dispatch were all actively tended tonight; the max-reduction over a buffer is none of their turf — it is fa-fmax's own headline consumer, a pure composition on the activation lane fam-relu opened.
toolchain_free_claim: Honest floor: a Form recipe proven four-way (Go=Rust=TS=fkwu) through validate.sh (bash) PLUS an on-M4 execution witness (form-macho -> ld -dylib -> dlopen, no rustc/clang in the compute). bin-go is the bootstrap that runs the flattener/emitter. Platform rows (mac four-way + on-hardware observed; windows/android form-cli pending) per docs/coherence-substrate/standard-receipt.form.
next_stones: (1) fam-softmax over ll-buffer end-to-end on bytes: fam-max-loop gives m = max_j x[j]; then a subtract-exp-sum-normalize pass (subtract m, exp via the range-reduced Horner now that fam-exp-reduce is on the lane, sum-reduce like fam-dot-loop, divide) — the full numerically-stable softmax the attention block needs. (2) fam-clamp using fa-fmin+fa-fmax over a pooled (lo,hi) — the last named fmax/fmin consumer. (3) fam-gelu/fam-silu (exp + fmul + the pooled poly). (4) stage matvec + bias + fam-relu over ll-buffer for a real MLP layer end-to-end on bytes.
witness_state: pulse.coherencycoin.com at open: overall STRAINED, 0 recorded silences. Brick is host-independent (Form recipe + band + witness + receipt + manifest row); no deploy taken.
